info.name: Ringotel Admin API
info.description:
### About Admin API

This Admin API allows you to administer your Ringotel Shell account from your backend.

### Transport

This Admin API uses **JSON** as its communication format.

You can use HTTP as a transport protocol.

The server is listening to the HTTP requests over the SSL/TLS protocol on the 443 port.

### Authentication and Authorization

Use API Key generated in your Ringotel Shell admin portal as a Bearer token to authenticate requests on the server. Read more about Ringotel API Settings [here](https://ringotel.atlassian.net/l/cp/jazgxjQ3).

All API calls should be made to the following URL:

`https://shell.ringotel.co/api`

### Requests format

The requests format includes `method` and `params` fields.  
The successful response from the server includes the `result` object (or array of objects in case of multiple results). In case of error, the `error` object will be returned.

### Example

**Request:**

``` json
{
    “method”: “getBranch”, 
    “params”: { 
        "id":"16029679507926612345",
        "orgid":"16029670196176812345" 
    }
}

 ```

**Response:**

``` json
{ 
    “result”: { 
        "id": "16029679507926612345", 
        "accountid": "16029670196176812345", 
        "created": 1602967950792, 
        "provision": {...}, 
        "domain": "mydomain", 
        ...
    }
}

 ```

**Last revision:**

April 02, 2024.

item.name: Organizations

request.description:
#### Get organizations

Returns the list of all organizations created under your Ringotel account

---

#### Parameters

_none_

---

#### Results

###### **`id`** **string**

Organization ID.

###### **`created`** **int**

Organization creation timestamp in unix-time format

###### **`domain`** **string**

Organization domain

###### **`name`** **string**

Organization name

###### **`admlogin`** **string**

Organization admin login

###### **`options`** **int**

Reserved parameter

`params` **object**

Additional organization settings.

- `admperms` **int**. Allow billable changes for the organization admin, such as create/import/activate/delete users and connections.
- `emailcc` **array**. Send welcome emails to these CC addresses.
- `tags` **array**. Organization tags.
- `hidePassInEmail` **boolean**. Do not send users' passwords in plain text.
- `lang` **string**. Wlecome email language. Provide ISO 639 alpha-2 language code, e.g. "de". Default "en".
    

###### **`packageid`** **int**

Organization package ID. Defines the features available to the users of this organization.

\* see possible values below

###### **`region`** **string**

Organization region where it was created

\* see possible values below

###### **`status`** **int**

Organization status

\* see possible values below

---

###### _**possible values for**_ `_**packageid**_`

| id | package |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Essentials |
| 2 | Pro |

###### _**possible values for**_ `_**region**_`

| id | region |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | US East |
| 2 | US West |
| 3 | Europe (Frankfurt) |
| 4 | Asia Pacific (Singapore) |
| 5 | Europe (London) |
| 6 | India |
| 7 | Australia |
| 8 | Europe (Dublin |
| 9 | Canada (Central) |
| 10 | South Africa |

###### _**possible values for**_ `_**status**_`

| id | status |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | Disabled |
| 1 | Enabled |

---

#### Get organization

Returns an organization settings.

---

#### Parameters

###### **`id`** **string**

(Required) Organization ID.

---

#### Result

###### **`created`** **int**

Unix timestamp when an organization was created.

###### **`domain`** **string**

Organization domain.

###### **`name`** **string**

Organization name.

###### **`admlogin`** **string**

Organization admin login.

###### **`options`** **int**

Additional organization options.

`params` **object**

Additional organization settings.

- `admperms` **int**. Allow billable changes for the organization admin, such as create/import/activate/delete users and connections. 1 - allow, 0 - restrict.
- `emailcc` **array**. Send welcome emails to these CC addresses.
- `tags` **array**. Organization tags.
- `hidePassInEmail` **boolean**. Do not send users' passwords in plain text.
- `lang` **string**. Welcome email language. Provide ISO 639 alpha-2 language code, e.g. "de". Default "en".
    

###### **`packageid`** **int**

The organization package. It sets the features available to the users.

\* see possible values below

###### **`id`** **string**

Organization ID.

###### **`region`** **string**

Organization region.

\* see possible values below

###### **`status`** **int**

Organization status.

\* see possible values below

---

###### _**possible values for**_ `_**packageid**_`

| id | package |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Essentials |
| 2 | Pro |

###### _**possible values for**_ `_**region**_`

| id | region |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | US East |
| 2 | US West |
| 3 | Europe (Frankfurt) |
| 4 | Asia Pacific (Singapore) |
| 5 | Europe (London) |
| 6 | India |
| 7 | Australia |
| 8 | Europe (Dublin) |
| 9 | Canada (Central) |
| 10 | South Africa |

###### _**possible values for**_ `_**status**_`

| id | status |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | Disabled |
| 1 | Enabled |

---

#### Create a new organization

---

#### Parameters

###### **`name`** **string**

(Required) Organization name.

###### **`domain`** **string**

(Required) Unique organization domain.

###### **`region`** string

(Required) Region ID. Choose the region closest to your users location. You won't be able to change it later.

_\* see possible values below_

###### **`admlogin`** **string**

Organization admin login (email address) to access the admin portal to manage that specific organization.

###### **`admpassw`** **string**

Organization admin password to access the admin portal to manage that specific organization.

`params` **object**

Additional organization settings.

- `admperms` **int**. Allow billable changes for the organization admin, such as create/import/activate/delete users and connections.
- `emailcc` **array**. Send welcome emails to these CC addresses.
- `tags` **array**. Organization tags.
- `hidePassInEmail` **boolean**. Do not send users' passwords in plain text.
- `lang` **string**. Wlecome email language. Provide ISO 639 alpha-2 language code, e.g. "de". Default "en".
    

###### **`packageid`** **int**

The organization package. Determines the features available to the users

\* see possible values below

---

###### **possible values for** **`packageid`**

| **id** | **package** |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Essentials |
| 2 | Pro |

---

###### **possible values for** **`region`**

| id | region |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | US East |
| 2 | US West |
| 3 | Europe (Frankfurt) |
| 4 | Asia Pacific (Singapore) |
| 5 | Europe (London) |
| 6 | India |
| 7 | Australia |
| 8 | Europe (Dublin) |
| 9 | Canada (Central) |
| 10 | South Africa |

---

### **Result**

###### **`id`** string

Organization ID

---

#### Update organization parameters

---

#### Parameters

###### **`id`** **string**

(Required) Organization ID to update.

###### **`name`** **string**

Organization name.

###### **`admlogin`** **string**

Organization admin login (email address) to access the admin portal to manage that specific organization.

###### **`admpassw`** **string**

Organization admin password to access the admin portal to manage that specific organization.

`params` **object**

Additional organization settings.

- `admperms` **int**. Access level for the organization admin ([learn more](https://ringotel.atlassian.net/l/cp/wD0tRs4v)):
    
    - 0 - Supervisor
    - 1 - IT Admin
    - 2 - Manager
- `emailcc` **array**. Send welcome emails to these CC addresses.
- `tags` **array**. Organization tags.
- `hidePassInEmail` **boolean**. Do not send users' passwords in plain text.
- `lang` **string**. Wlecome email language. Provide ISO 639 alpha-2 language code, e.g. "de". Default "en".
    

###### **`packageid`** **int**

The package you want to use for your organization. Determines the functions available to the users

\* see possible values below

---

###### **possible values for** **`packageid`**

| **id** | **package** |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Essentials |
| 2 | Pro |

---

#### Result

This method returns no data.

item.description: Organizations unifies users from a single or multiple connected IP PBXs under a single domain. They also define a geographical region in which users will be created and later connect to. Organization administrators can be created to delegate partial or full administration of a specific organization.

request.description:
#### Create a new connection

---

#### Required Parameters

###### **`orgid`** **string**

Organization ID where you want to create a new connection.

###### **`name`** **string**

Connection name.

###### **`address`** **string**

Domain or IP address of the SIP server. Specify the SIP port after the colon, e.g. "pbx.example.com:5060".

`provision` **object.**

Defines softphone features and connection settings.

- `multitenant` **boolean**. Multi-tenant mode. You should send this option only if your PBX is multi-tenant and does not support local-domain parameter of the Contact SIP header.
- `protocol` **string**. Transport protocol. Possible values: `sip`, `sips`, `sip-tcp`, `DNS-NAPTR`.
- `codecs` **array**. Defines the list of audio codecs enabled for this connection. Each object in the list must contain parameters `codec` and `frame`, where:
    - `codec` **string**. Name of the audio codec. Possible values: `G.711 Alaw`, `G.711 Ulaw`, `G.729`, `Opus`.
    - `frame` **int**. Audio codec framesize (in ms), e.g. 20.
- `regexpires` **int**. SIP re-registration timeout.
- `dtmfmode` **string**. Selected DTMF mode for this connection. Possible vlaues: `rfc2833`, `inband`, `sip info`.
    

---

#### Result

###### **`id`** **string**

New connection ID.

---

#### Update a connection

---

#### Required Parameters

###### **`id`** **string**

Connection ID.

###### **`orgid`** **string**

Organization ID.

---

#### Result

This method returns no data.

---

#### **Delete a connection**

This action deletes all users associated with this connection and their data.

---

#### Parameters

###### **`id`** **string**

Connection ID.

###### **`orgid`** **string**

Organization ID.

---

#### Result

List of deleted `userid`s associated with the deleted connection.

---

#### **Set the status of a connection**

---

#### Parameters

###### **`id`** **string**

Connection ID.

###### **`orgid`** **string**

Organization ID.

###### **`status`** **int**

Connection status.

\* see possible values below

---

| Status | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | disabled |
| 1 | enabled |

---

#### **Result**

This method returns no data.

---

#### **Returns available provisioning options specific to an organization's region**

---

#### Parameters

###### **`orgid`** **string**

The ID of your organization

---

#### Result

`devices` **array**. List of available transport protocols and associated SIP ports for generating terminal passwords.

`tones` **object**. Contains lists of available Ringback and Progress tones.

`services` **array**. Contains a list of available integrations.

---

item.description:
Connections are provisioning templates that contain PBX connection details and features configured for users created in a connection profile.

A connection has the following parameters:

###### **`id`** **string**

Connection ID.

###### **`country`** **string**

Default country code for this connection.

###### **`address`** **string**

SIP server domain or IP address.

###### **`created`** **int**

Time when the connection was created. This parameter is read-only.

\* in [unix-time](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unix_time) format

###### **`domain`** **string**

Organization domain.

###### **`name`** **string**

###### **Connection name**

###### **`provision`** object

Defines softphone features and connection settings.

- `displayname` **string**. Connection display name.
    
- `multitenant` **boolean**. Enable multi-tenant mode. You should check this option only if your PBX is multi-tenant and does not support local-domain parameter of the Contact SIP header.
    
- `sms` **int**. Enable SMS service. Possible values:
    
    - 0 - SMS are disabled;
        
    - 1 - enable SMS via SIP SIMPLE;
        
    - 2 - enable SMS via API;
        
    - 3 - enable SMS via Integrations;
        
- `protocol` **string**. Transport protocol. Possible values: `sip`, `sips`, `sip-tcp`, `DNS-NAPTR`.
    
- `codecs` **array**. Defines the list of audio codecs enabled for this connection. Each object in the list must contain parameters `codec` and `frame`, where:
    
    - `codec` **string**. Name of the audio codec. Possible values: `G.711 Alaw`, `G.711 Ulaw`, `G.729`, `Opus`.
        
    - `frame` **int**. Audio codec framesize (in ms), e.g. 20.
        
- `dtmfmode` **string**. Selected DTMF mode for this connection. Possible vlaues: `rfc2833`, `inband`, `sip info`.
    
- `nosrtp` **boolean**. Disable SRTP if `protocol` value is set to `sips`.
    
- `noverify` **boolean**. Do not verify server certificate if `protocol` value is set to `sips`.
    
- `regexpires` **int**. SIP re-registration timeout.
    
- `dialtimeout` **int**. The ringing timeout is the maximum amount of time that an incoming call rings a user's device before it is canceled or is routed to voicemail.
    
- `sipheaders` **array**. Specify additional SIP headers that Ringotel server will send in INVITE to the PBX during outgoing calls. Each header object must contain parameters `name` and `value`, where:
    
    - `name` **string**. Header name.
        
    - `value` **string**. Header value.
        
- `tones` **object**. Ringback and Progress tones selection. The object may contain the following key/values:
    
    - Key `Ringback`. Possible values: `United States`, `Germany`, `United Kingdom`.
        
    - Key `Ringback2`. Possible values: `Ringback 1`, `Ringback 2`.
        
    - Key `Progress`. Possible values: `Progress 1`, `Progress 2`.
        
- `proxy` **object**. Outbound (SIP) Proxy settings.
    
    - `paddr` **string**. Proxy server address.
        
    - `pauth` **string**. Proxy Auth. username.
        
    - `ppass` **string**. Proxy Auth password.
        
- `httpsproxy` **object**. HTTPS proxy settings.
    
    - `address` **string**. HTTPS proxy server address.
        
- `ipcheck` **boolean**. Enable ACL to allow users to connect only from specific IPs/networks.
    
- `iptable` **array**. List of objects that must contain net and mask paramters, where:
    
    - `net` **string**. IP address, e.g. 192.168.1.100.
        
    - `mask` **string**. Subnet Mask, e.g. 255.255.255.0.
        
- `inboundFormat` **string**. Regormat inbound numbers according to the default country code specified in the `country` paramter. Possible values: `e164`, `national`, `national0`. By default, an empty string is used, which disables reformatting of incoming numbers.
    
- `maxregs` **int**. Maximum number of registrations per user.
    
- `blfs` **array**. List of BLF indicators. Each object must contain the following keys:
    
    - `number` **string**. BLF subscription number of the PBX feature.
        
    - `title` **string**. Name of the BLF indicator.
        
- `speeddial` **array**. List of Speed Dial buttons. Each object must contain the following keys:
    
    - `number` **string**. Speed dial number.
        
    - `title` **string**. Speed dial button label.
        
    - `gsm` **boolean**. Dial this number using the native cellular dialer (mobiles only).
        
- `emergency` **array**. List of emergency numbers. Calls to emergency numbers are made through the native cellular dialer on mobile devices and do not appear on the Speed ​​Dial or Contacts tabs.
    
- `callpark` **object**. Visual Call Parking settings.
    
    - `slots` **array**. List of available call parking slots. Each object must contain the following keys:
        
        - `slot` **string**. PBX extension number of a parking slot.
            
        - `alias` **string**. Parking slot label.
            
    - `subscribe` **string**. BLF subscription prefix. Added to the call park slot to subscribe for slot's status.
        
    - `retrieve` **string**. Call Park retrieve prefix. Added to the call park slot to retrieve a call.
        
    - `park` **string**. Call Park prefix. Added to the call park slot to park a call.
        
- `custompages` **array**. Allows to open custom web pages inside the app. Each object in the list must contain the following keys:
    
    - `title` **string**. Webpage label, e.g. "Home".
        
    - `url` **string**. Webpage URL, e.g. "[https://ringotel.co"](https://ringotel.co).
        
    - You can use the following parameters in the URL:
        
        - $userid$ - softphone user ID;
            
        - $domain$ - organization domain;
            
        - $number$ - extension number;
            
        - $username$ - username;
            
        - $email$ - user email address;
            
        - $mobile$ - user mobile number;
            
- `plength` **int**. Terminal password length.
    
- `popup` **int**. Enable screen pop-ups. Possible values:
    
    - 0 - Enable screen pop-up, but do not open on incoming call;
        
    - 1 - Open screen pop-up on incoming call;
        
    - 2 - Open screen pop-up after answering an incoming call;
        
    - Pass a null value to disable screen pop-ups;
        
- `application` **string**. If `popup` is enabled, a web URL or an absolute path to the application executable file.
    
- `norec` **boolean**. Disable call recording feature.
    
- `nostates` **boolean**. Restrict users from changing their state from the app, such as Online/DND/At the desk.
    
- `novideo` **boolean**. Disable video calls.
    
- `nochats` **boolean**. Restrict users from using internal chat feature and create new chats.
    
- `norecents` **boolean**. Disable call history syncing in iPhone Recents. If enabled, this option disables call history syncing in iPhone Recents and hides the "Show calls in iPhone Recents" option from the app's settings.
    
- `sms2email` **boolean**. Send missed SMS, voicemail and call notifications to email.
    
- `sms2phone` **boolean**. Send SMS to connected SIP endpoints.
    
- `visibility` **int**. Users visibility. Possible values:
    
    - 0 - **Default:** users within this connection can see other users of the same connection and users of any other non-supervisory connections.
        
    - 1 - **Private:** users within this connection can see other users of the same connection and users of Default connections.
        
    - 2 - **Standalone:** users within this connection can't see other users from any connection including their own connection.
        
    - 3 - **Supervisory:** users within this connection can see other users from any connection including their own connection. Supervisory users can't be seen by non-supervisory users.
        
- `internal` **boolean** (_**deprecated).**_ Do not route internal calls through PBX.
    
- `internalRouting` **number**. Choose how to route internal calls between the extensions.
    
    - 1 - Route internal calls through PBX only. The calls will not be routed through Ringotel under any circumstances.
        
    - 2 - Route internal calls through PBX if possible. If both users are connected to the same PBX (same connection or same server address in the settings of different connections).
        
    - 3 - Do not route internal calls through PBX.
        
- `nologmc` **boolean**. Don't log missed calls in the calls history.
    
- `nologae` **boolean**. Don't log "Answered elsewhere" in the calls history.
    
- fallback object.
    
- `pcdelay` **boolean**. Delay calls to desktop app if the "At the desk" mode is enabled.
    
- `calldelay` **int**. "At the desk" status call delay.
    
- `paging` **boolean**. Enable Visual Paging. If visual paging is enabled, Ringotel will attempt to record paging messages and deliver them to the users softphone as voice recordings.
    
- `app` **object**.
    
    - `g711` **boolean**. Use G711 audio codec instead of OPUS. Enabling the OPUS audio codec between the softphone apps and a softphone server improves call quality on low bandwidth/congested networks, but may cause small audio delays.
        
- `features` **string**. Specify "pbx" to utilize Call Forwarding, Voicemail, DND, and Call Waiting features on the PBX instead of Ringotel.
    
- `forwarding` **object**. Call Forwarding settings.
    
    - `cfuon` **string**. The feature code used to activate CF No Answer/Unavailable, e.g. \*52.
        
    - `cfuoff` **string**. The feature code used to deactivate CF No Answer/Unavailable, e.g. \*53
        
    - `cfboff` **string**. The feature code used to deactivate CF Busy, e.g. \*91
        
    - `cfon` **string**. The feature code used to activate CF, e.g. \*72
        
    - `cfbon` **string**. The feature code used to activate CF Busy, e.g. \*90
        
    - `cfoff` **string**. The feature code used to deactivate CF, e.g. \*73
        
- `vmail` **object**.
    
    - `ext` **string**. The feature code used to dial voicemail, e.g. \*97
        
    - `mess` **string**. New voicemail message.
        
    - `unsolicited` **boolean**. Allow unsolicited notifications.
        
    - `off` **string**. The feature code used to deactivate Voicemail.
        
    - `on` **string**. The feature code used to activate Voicemail.
        
    - `spref` **string**. BLF subscription prefix.
        
- `dnd` **object**. Do Not Disturb settings.
    
    - `off` **string**. The feature code used to deactivate DND mode, e.g. \*79
        
    - `on` **string**. The feature code used to activate DND mode, e.g. \*78
        
- `callwaiting` **object**. Call Waiting settings.
    
    - `off` **string**. The feature code used to deactivate Call Waiting, e.g. \*71
        
    - `on` **string**. The feature code used to activate Call Waiting, e.g. \*70
        
- `acceptedCNs` **string**. Specify Common Name (CN) of an SSL/TLS certificate. You can specify multiple CNs separated by comma. Alternatively, specify "\*" to allow any certificate.
    
- `extst` **boolean**. Route users' calls to their extension number through PBX.
    
- `phonebook` **object**. External XML Phonebook provisioninig settings.
    
    - `url` **string**. Valid URL that points to an external XML Phonebook.
        
    - `verifycert` **boolean**. Verify certificate. Please ensure that the web server has valid SSL certificate.
        

`huntgroup` **object**. Calls distribution group (SIP Trunk Provider connections only).

- `users` **array**. List of user IDs assigned to the hunting group.
    
- `delay` **number.** Call delay for sequential and random distribution modes.
    
- `mode` **number**. Method for distributing incoming calls to the assigned users. 0 - parallel, 1 - sequential, 2 - random.

# Get User Logs

This endpoint is used to retrieve user logs based on the provided user ID and domain.

## Request Body

- Method (string) - The method to be executed, in this case, "getUserLogs".
    
- Params (object)
    
    - userid (string) - The unique identifier of the user for whom the logs are to be retrieved.
        
    - domain (string) - The domain for which the logs are to be retrieved.
        

## Response

The response contains an array of objects of user logs.

## Retrieve log files

To retrive a log file, use the returned link in the "url" paramter.

item.description:
Create Ringotel users and connect them to the corresponding PBX extensions.

When you create users, they are automatically added to the contact list of every activated Ringotel user of a given organization (exceptions may depend on the visibility option set in the connection settings).

For users who do not use Ringotel softphones, but who have registered directly to the PBX using an IP phone or third-party softphone, Ringotel allows automatic provisioning of BLF contacts by creating **unactivated** users.

request.description:
#### **Get the list of blocked contacts of a particular organization.**

---

#### Parameters

`orgid` **string**

Organization ID.

---

#### **Results**

###### **`id`** **string**

Contact ID.

###### `blocked` int

Timestamp when a contact was blocked.

\* in [unix-time](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unix_time) format

###### **`blockby`** string

Organization ID or User ID who blocked a contact.

###### **`createdby`** string

Organization ID or Connection ID (if the `branchid` parameter was passed when importing contacts) where the contact was created.

###### **`created`** **int**

Timestamp when a contact was created.

\* in [unix-time](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unix_time) format

###### **`name`** **string**

Contact full name.

###### **`imgdate`** **int**

Indicates whether the contact's avatar is uploaded.

###### **`consent`** **int**

Reserved parameter.

###### **`status`** **int**

Current contact status. Status 4 indicates that the contact was created.

###### **`info`** object

Additional contact details.

- `position` **string.** Position in a company.
    
- **`organization`** **string.** Company name.
    
- **`department`** **string.** Department name.
    
- `about` **string**. Additional contact information.
    
- **`email`** **array**. The list of contact email adresses.
    
- `mobile` **array**. The list of contact mobile numbers.
    
- `phone` **array**. The list of contact phone numbers.
    
- `fax` **array**. The list of contact fax numbers.
    

---

#### **Block external contact by ID or Phone number**

---

#### Parameters

`orgid` **string**

Organization ID.

`branchid` string

Connection ID. Required only when blocking contacts that were created under a specific connection

`id` **string**

Contact ID (for existing contacts). Optionally use `phone` parameter to block a new contact that doesn't have an ID yet.

`blocked` boolean

Sets contact status as blocked/unblocked.

`about` **string**

Contact description.

---

#### **Results**

This method doesn't return any data

---

request.description:
#### Import external contacts

These contacts become available to all users within the organization or a specific connection (if `branchid` is specified).

---

#### Parameters

###### **`orgid`** **string**

Organization ID.

`branchid` **string**

(Optional) Connection ID.

###### **`list`** array

The list of contacts to be imported.

- `name` **string**. Contact full name. Alternatively, you can specify `firstname` and `lastname`.
    
- `info` object. The info object contains contact information.
    
    - `email` **array**. The list of contact email adresses (e.g. "[john.doe@example.com](https://mailto:john.doe@example.com)").
        
    - `mobile` **array**. The list of contact mobile phone numbers.
        
    - `phone` **array**. The list of contact landline phone numbers.
        
    - `fax` **array**. The list of contact fax numbers.
        
    - `sip` **array**. The list of contact SIP numbers.
        
    - `organization` **string.** Company name.
        
    - `position` **string.** Position in a company.
        
    - `department` **string.** Department name.
        
    - `about` **string.** Extra information about a contact.
        

---

#### Results

Returns the list of created contacts.

###### **`name`** **string**

Contact First- and Lastname.

###### **`id`** **string**

New Contact ID.

###### **`status`** **int**

Contact status. Status 4 indicates that a contact has been added.

###### **`info`** object

Includes contact detail information.

- `email` **array**. The list of contact email adresses (e.g. "[john.doe@example.com](https://mailto:john.doe@example.com)").
    
- `mobile` **array**. The list of contact mobile phone numbers.
    
- `phone` **array**. The list of contact landline phone numbers.
    
- `fax` **array**. The list of contact fax numbers.
    
- `sip` **array**. The list of contact SIP numbers.
    
- `organization` **string.** Company name.
    
- `position` **string.** Position in a company.
    
- `department` **string.** Department name.
    
- `about` **string.** Extra information about a contact.
    

---

SMS trunk ID.

Unix timestamp when an SMS trunk was created.

###### **`service`** **string**

Service name, e.g. "Twilio".

###### **`name`** **string**

SMS trunk name.

###### **`users`** **string**

The list of user IDs who are assigned to the SMS trunk.

`country` **string**

Country code (ALPHA-2) that is used for re-formatting phone numbers.

`inboundFormat` **string**

Reformat **inbound** numbers to the specified format. Possible values: `national`, `national0`, `e164`. Pass an empty string to disable

`outboundFormat` **string**

Reformat **outbound** numbers to the specified format. Possible values: `national`, `international`, `e164`. Pass an empty string to disable.

`sendName` **boolean**

Send user names in messages.

`sessionTimeout` **int**

If `sessionTimeout` > 0, all outgoing chats initiated by the assigned users will create private chats for the specified time period. If 0, each new conversation (incoming/outgoing) will create a group chat if multiple users are assigned to this number.

`autoreply` **array**

List of auto-reply prompts. Each item in a list must be an object with key/value pairs, where:

- `key` **string**. Case-insensitive keyword. Specify \* (asterisk) to set initial auto-response.
- `value` **string**. An auto-response text sent to end user after a keyword request.
    

`optout` **object**.

10DLC optout keywork and auto-reply message settings.

- `keyword` **string**. Case-insensitive Opt-Out keyword, .e.g "STOP".
- `autoreply` **string**. An auto-response sent to end user after an Opt-Out request.
    

###### **`status`** **string**

SMS trunk status

\* see possible values below

---

possible values for `status`

| Status | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | enabled |
| 1 | disabled |

---

request.description:
#### **Create a new SMS trunk**

---

#### Parameters

###### **`id`** **string**

SMS trunk ID.

`orgid` **string**

Organization ID.

###### **`number`** **string**

SMS trunk phone number.

###### **`service`** **string**

Service ID, e.g. "Telnyx".

###### **`name`** **string**

SMS trunk name.

###### **`users`** **string**

List of user IDs assigned to the SMS trunk. Defines who will send and receive SMS through this phone number. If multiple users are assigned, all new outgoing or incoming messages will create a group chat where all assigned users can chat with the external contact and collaborate as a team.

`country` **string**

Country code (ALPHA-2) that is used for re-formatting phone numbers.

`inboundFormat` **string**

Reformat **inbound** numbers to the specified format. Possible values: `national`, `national0`, `e164`. Pass an empty string to disable

`outboundFormat` **string**

Reformat **outbound** numbers to the specified format. Possible values: `national`, `international`, `e164`. Pass an empty string to disable.

`sendName` **boolean**

Send user names in messages.

`sessionTimeout` **int**

If `sessionTimeout` > 0, all outgoing chats initiated by the assigned users will create private chats for the specified time period. If 0, each new conversation (incoming/outgoing) will create a group chat if multiple users are assigned to this number.

`optout` object

Herer you can specify a keyword to trigger the opt-out request and auto-reply message.

- `keyword` **string**. A keyword to trigger the opt-out request. The keyword is case-insensitive.
- `autoreply` **string**. A confirmation message to acknowledge the request and provide further information, such as a statement confirming that they have been unsubscribed.
    

`autoreply` **array**

List of auto-reply prompts. Each item in a list must be an object with key/value pairs, where:

- `key` **string**. Case-insensitive keyword. Specify \* (asterisk) to set initial auto-response.
- `value` **string**. An auto-response text sent to end user after a keyword request.
    

---

#### Results

###### **`id`** **string**

New SMS trunk ID.

---

---

#### Parameters

SMS trunk ID.

###### **`number`** **string**
